description: Business letter in Judaeo-Arabic. Relatively large and complete, but faded, and there is no address on verso. The sender has sent 8 dinars and 15 nutmegs (jawza) with Abū l-Faraj. Mentions Abū l-Munā. Encourages the sender to work on "emptying the shop" (takhliyat al-dukkān, a common phrase in Geniza documents whose meaning is not entirely clear). The sender may say that he does not wish to live in ʿAkkā any more (three lines from the bottom of recto). He has sent a mat (naṭʿ) for Saʿīd. Greetings to the elder 'al-Mumḥe.' Reused for Hebrew piyyuṭ on verso.
